texts.full_text: d elsewhere I remember reading years ago like revelations (alongside criticism by such other early influencers as Eric Bentley, Richard Gilman, Tom Driver, and Stanley Kauffmann). In “Drama Criticism: The Old Age of an Age-Old Profession,” printed in the STC program and available online, Brustein writes: People who judge the theatre have always been uneasy with one other. But in the long-running contention between the theatre reviewer and the drama critic, it is not often recognized that the two professionals are pursuing entirely separate paths. The reviewer is primarily interested in product, while the critic is more absorbed with process. The reviewer can turn out a notice between the falling of the curtain and the rising of the sun, while the critic normally has at least a week to revise, reenact, and redact first impressions. Brustein goes on to elaborate on this critical distinction. “The reviewer,” he says, “steers audiences towards shows thought worth the ticket price.” I think of this type of theater coverage as ratings by a solo product tester claiming Consumer Reports objectivity and authority. On the other hand, “the critic,” Brustein says, “is less concerned with sitting in judgment on a particular play than in trying to describe how it fits in a playwright’s artistic trajectory, in a company’s season, or in the history of dramatic literature.” I would add to that: the conscientious critic tries to describe how a particular play relates to the life we live and the world we live in. texts.full_text: nd independent component analysis, we do not carry out the extractioThe extraction of the desired signal from a linear mixture of unknown sources can be achieved with a blind extraction approach, which exploits the temporal structure of the desired signal. In a batch blind extraction, the linear mixture of the sources and the temporal structure of the desired signal have to be estimated. In this letter, a batch blind extraction algorithm with a linear estimation of the temporal structure of the desired signal and a nonlinear estimation of the linear mixture is developed. In the batch algorithm, a small and a large data sample are required. The algorithm is tested with the extraction of the desired signal from a linear mixture of four signals and with the extraction of a desired signal from a linear mixture of a large number of signals. The simulations show a good extraction performance and a good stability. The algorithm is an alternative to the batch algorithm with a linear estimation of the linear mixture and a nonlinear estimation of the temporal structure. In an estimation-theoretic framework, we discuss a robust scheme for the blind extraction of a desired source from a mixture of unknown signals. This is achieved by exploiting the temporal structure of the desired source. In the scheme, the unknown mixture is estimated by a linear estimation, and the unknown temporal structure is estimated by a linear estimation. In addition, the desired source is estimated by a nonlinear estimation. In the estimation, the estimation error is bounded, and the estimation is robust. Simulation results, in which the robust estimation scheme is compared to a nonlinear estimation scheme, illustrate the estimation and the robust estimation. This letter discusses a robust scheme for the estimation of a desired signal from a mixture of unknown sources, where the desired signal is a source with temporal structure. texts.full_text: To achieve a 3D effect on text, you can use a variety of tools and techniques. Here, I'll provide a step-by-step guide on how to create a 3D effect on text using Blender, a powerful and free 3D modeling software. ### Step-by-Step Guide to Create a 3D Effect on Text using Blender #### 1. Install Blender - Download and install Blender from the official website: [Blender.org](https://www.blender.org/download/). #### 2. Open Blender and Create a New Text - Open Blender and create a new project. - Press `Shift + A` to add a new object. - Select `Text` to create a new text object. #### 3. Enter Text - In the text object, type the text you want to create the 3D effect for. #### 4. Adjust Text Properties - In the `Text` panel on the right-hand side, you can adjust the font, size, and other text properties. #### 5. Convert Text to Mesh - With the text object selected, press `Alt + C` and choose `Convert to Mesh` to convert the text to a mesh object. #### 6. Apply Transformations - Press `A` to select all, then press `Ctrl + A` and choose `All Transformations` to apply any transformations to the text. #### 7. Tilt the Text - Press `R` to rotate the text. - Press `Z` to rotate around the Z-axis. - Enter `45` to tilt the text 45 degrees. #### 8. Expand the Text - Press `S` to scale the text. - Press `Z` to scale along the Z-axis. - Enter a value to expand the text. For a cylindrical effect, you might want to scale it significantly. #### 9. texts.full_text: l Daisy Miller by Henry James I only recently started reading Henry James. I could not stand him in graduate school, when I was in my 20’s, and never finished him when he was assigned, but twenty years on, I find much to enjoy in his work. I suspect he may be someone you have to grow into; I don’t think he has much to say to the young; one needs more life experience before he can be appreciated. But why shouldn’t living long come with a few rewards? Daisy Miller may be a good choice for those new to Henry James, as it is relatively short and accessible compared to some of his denser works like or . The novella tells the story of Daisy Miller, an American girl traveling in Europe with her family. She is seen by many of the expatriates she meets as brash, naive, and lacking in social graces, but also as refreshingly free-spirited. James explores themes of cultural clash, the constraints of social norms, and the complexities of personal freedom through Daisy's interactions. The narrative is primarily told from the perspective of Winterbourne, a young American who has lived abroad for many years and finds himself both fascinated and troubled by Daisy’s behavior. While can be read as a critique of European snobbery or a commentary on American innocence, it also delves into deeper questions about individuality and conformity. It's a good starting point to understand James's style

texts.full_text: at HERA [^1]' --- Introduction {#introduction .unnumbered} ------------ The theoretical treatment of heavy quark production has undergone much re-examination recently. A basic question is whether, or not, the charm quark (or bottom quark) should be included in the initial state, with its own parton density function (PDF). Traditionally, there have been two approaches used, which we shall call the Fixed-Flavor-Number (FFN) scheme and the Zero-Mass Variable-Flavor-Number (ZM-VFN) scheme. In the FFN scheme [1], heavy quarks are included in an effective theory as part of a redefinition or modification to standard DIS cross sections that includes higher order terms calculated from perturbation theories. On other hand, ZM-VFN scheme considers massless point particles and only accounts for the radiative correction effects associated with introducing finite mass (massive calculations) [2] which gives results in better agreement at small $x$ regime where gluon density peaks compared to FFN approach but still has challenges near threshold regions or on certain observables. One of main advances beyond these two traditional methods is General-Mass Variable Flavor Number Scheme(GM VFN )scheme that applies when considering fully integrated cross sections such as total structure function but keeps heavy quarks in the initial state taking into account their masses throughout calculations for all $Q^2$ above charm quark threshold. This provides a consistent treatment across whole kinematical range hence it is expected to give superior results specially near mass thresholds where other methods tend struggle due inconsistencies like "zero-mass" requirement when actually they have finite lifetimes thus necessarily

texts.full_text: a running story but a story of life and that is why I have been writing my book for the last year. My goal for writing this book is for the reader to be able to filter out the running and realize it just takes a few changes and an open mind to overcome bad circumstances. My book is close to being complete but as I left the stage the other day and finished telling my story I now know exactly how it ends. My journey was not just as a speaker this week but I had the rare opportunity to race with the Tarahumura, talk to some inspiring athletes including Gold Medalist Billy Mills, Nike sponsored Olympic trials runner Alvina Begay, world Champion swimmer and olympic Trial hopeful Tere Zacher, and Ironman finisher Caroline Mei-Ling Sekaquaptewa. I learned so much by hearing them speak and it is amazing the inspiration you can take away from a forum like the Hopi tribe put on. I would encourage anyone interested in running or looking for some help finding inspiration to sign up and attend next year. The raw emotion of the attendees and the inspiration of watching so many go out and run really made me question what has been holding me back when racing. texts.full_text: that London's economic decline could result in a loss of income and wealth across the country. It highlights the significant difference in productivity between London and the rest of the UK, indicating that London's economic status is distinct from that of other regions. This statement by John Redwood's Diary is an argument against the idea of taxing the rich out of London to bring its figures down to nearer the national average. The text suggests that levelling up should not be achieved by diminishing London's wealth but rather by spreading it more widely around the country. It acknowledges that there are substantial gaps in wealth and income between London and other parts of the UK, but it emphasizes the importance of maintaining London's economic vibrancy to ensure the overall prosperity of the nation. The text points out that London has a higher concentration of small and competing companies per 1000 residents than any other part of the country. This results in greater private sector wealth and income per person compared to elsewhere. The argument is made that London's high cost of living and doing business, despite these benefits, does not deter people from moving there. They are attracted by the promise of better-paid jobs and access to a more affluent customer base. In conclusion, the text argues against the idea of reducing London's economic status to bring it closer to the national average. Instead, it suggests that policies should focus on spreading wealth and income more evenly across the country while maintaining London's distinct economic position. texts.full_text: ers, Stevers began auditioning guitarists for the reformation of Frijid Pink. A keyboard player was also recruited. Over the course of the next five years, this new lineup would play a dozen or so gigs, such as local venues and street fairs, but, without proper management, never had any steady work or did any touring. The focus shifted to basement recording and piecing an album together, which was released in March 2011 on the Repertoire label. This album, available in the U.S. as an import, is composed of re-recorded renditions of songs from the group's previous albums and new original music. In late 2011, the lead singer/guitarist/engineer and the keyboard player were replaced, thus ending the longest contiguous line-up in the band's history. Recording and performing continued in a similar fashion with this latest lineup, and an EP was released in late spring of 2012. An EP, entitled "Taste of Pink" was released in early 2017, featuring 3 new songs and a newly recorded version of House of the Rising Sun. The three new songs featured on the EP were included in the 2018 album On the Edge. In 2013, Frijid Pink was inducted into the Michigan Rock and Roll Legends Hall of Fame. texts.full_text: a technological antigen delivery platform developed by the Institut Pasteur and the Academy of Sciences of the Czech Republic. Bordetella bronshiseptica Adenylcyclase (CyaA) protein has been genetically engineered to allow antigen insertion to activate the immune system against cells with the inserted antigen. This activation is carried out by means of specific cells of the immune response, the dendritic cells (DC), using signaling pathways which allow to initiate a cellular and humoral response (Th1 type response). This type of response is ideal for the development of prophylactic but also therapeutic vaccines. The vaccines produced with the CyaA vector are chimeric recombinant proteins consisting of the CyaA protein and the selected antigen, inserted by genetic engineering. ​ When injected into the animal, the CyaA vector naturally delivers the antigen inserted directlWhen injected into the animal, the CyaA vector naturally delivers the antigen inserted d

texts.full_text: The economy requires wise, decisive and consistent management if it is to avoid crisis and instead improve, bringing to Brazilians the jobs and higher incomes the vast majority of them need. The question is whether Lula, long-time presidential contender of the left and finally successful, can prove a catalyst of reform and strengthening, rather than the architect of collapse. What are Lula's priorities? They are the right ones. Improving the standard of living of Brazilians, beginning with the poor -- of whom Brazil has tens of millions. How to go about it is Lula's question. Now, on his tour to visit Bush earlier this week and Fox on Wednesday, in Mexico, he may be learning. The United States wants to talk trade with Brazil. When Bush met Lula earlier this week, so did Robert Zoellick, the U.S. Trade Representative, and the latter held a separate meeting, too with Lula. The bait for Zoellick, a committed free trader, and for the United States is that Brazil should commit itself to the Free Trade Area of the Americas, a plan first mooted by the first President Bush to spread free trade throughout the hemisphere. Brazil has long been wary of it. The reasons for that are various. Brazil is a big country, almost as big as the United States, and sees itself as no one's sidekick. Its distrust of the United States is strong. Concrete actions have not helped. Brazil exports steel and didn't like the increase in U.S. steel tariffs this year. Brazil exports agricultural goods and decried the U.S. farm bill this April that put in place high subsidies for U.S. farmers over the next 10 years. The outgoing U.S. Treasury Secretary, Paul O'Neill, spoke of loans going to South America that ended up in Swiss bank accounts. His words caused offense in Brazil. The United States is seen in Brazil as a superpower that plays dirty and has hegemonic intentions. texts.full_text: 2-(4,4-difluoro-1-thiocarbamoylbutoxy) substituent was introduced at the xanthine C8 position to generate 12-(2-(4,4-difluoro-1-thiocarbamoylbutoxy)ethyl)-8-(3-fluoropropyl)-3-methyl-1,4,8,12-tetraazatricyclo[10.2.2.11,4]dodecane-3,10-diol (27) and, thus, to explore whether this functionalization can decrease in vitro metabolism. We found that the use of higher enzyme and substrate concentrations, higher assay temperatures, and longer incubation times, respectively, all significantly reduced the intrinsic clearance (CLint) of all three compounds. Although the introduction of the 2-(4,4-difluoro-1-thiocarbamoylbutoxy) substituent reduced the CLint of 27 by 77-fold in comparison to the 2-(4-ethoxybutoxy) analog, the metabolic stability of 27 was not sufficiently increased to enable the successful PET evaluation of 27 in rats. We therefore conclude that the microsomal stability of xanthine-derived A1AR ligands should be tested using enzyme and substrate concentrations of 0.20 mg/mL and 0.25-1.0 μM, respectively, and an incubation time of 10-15 min. A1AR ligands should, thus, be tested under comparable in vitro conditions in order to allow the valid comparison of their in vitro metabolic stability. texts.full_text: Case Analysis: The Lathrop Company v Chester P Lampert - Lease Agreement Overview: - Commenced on January 1, 1965 for a term of 50 years ending December 31, 2014 with an initial rent of $1,500 per month. - Rent Adjustment Clause: - The lease included provisions to adjust the monthly rental based on changes in Alaska's Wholesale Price Index (WPI) every five years. This was likely intended as a cost-of-living adjustment mechanism. - Termination and Litigation: - Disagreement over rent adjustments led to termination of part or all of the lease, resulting in litigation that reached Alaska’s Supreme Court. - Court's Ruling: - The court treated it procedurally without significant controversy due to clear terms. It emphasized judicial restraint by not second-guessing business decisions unless there was a legal violation. Key Considerations and Implications: 1. Rent Escalation Clauses: - These clauses aim for long-term stability, avoiding frequent renegotiations but can lead disputes if economic indices fluctuate unpredictably. 2. Judicial Discretion: - Courts generally avoid intervening in contract terms unless there's a breach or legal issue beyond interpretation. 3. Relevance Today: - The case may set precedent for lease adjustment clauses, especially regarding WPI usage versus modern indices like CPI (Consumer Price Index). 4

texts.full_text: example with the use of mathematical modelling, could help to prevent tumour regrowth and anaplastic transformation. Here, we present a model of the effect of radiotherapy on such tumours. Our objective is to explain observed delay of tumour regrowth for patients undergoing treatment. The proposed oedema-based modeling approach considers both tumor growth dynamics as well as associated peritumoral edematous changes, which are common in diffuse low-grade gliomas and can significantly impact clinical outcomes. By integrating these factors into a mathematical framework that simulates the effects of radiotherapy over time, we aim to provide insights on how treatment parameters might be optimized. Our model is built upon differential equations representing tumor cell proliferation rates influenced by radiation dose distribution within both tumorous tissue as well as surrounding edematous regions. The inclusion of oedema dynamics allows us not only a more accurate prediction regarding the immediate effects post-radiotherapy but also long-term outcomes concerning potential regrowth and transformation. To validate our model, we applied it to clinical cases where patients received radiotherapeutic interventions

texts.full_text: Physics, they used graphene, the world’s thinnest and strongest material, and made it magnetic. Graphene is a sheet of carbon atoms arranged in a chicken wire structure. In its pristine state, it exhibits no signs of the conventional magnetism usually associated with such materials as iron or nickel. This latest research led by Dr Irina Grigorieva and Professor Sir Andre Geim (one of the Nobel prize recipients) could prove crucial to the future of graphene in electronics. The Manchester researchers took nonmagnetic graphene and then either ‘peppered’ it with other nonmagnetic atoms like fluorine or removed some carbon atoms from the chicken wire. The empty spaces, called vacancies, and added atoms all turned out to be magnetic, exactly like atoms of, for example, iron. The researchers found that, to behave as magnetic atoms, defects must be far away from each other and their concentration should be low. If many defects are added to graphene, they reside too close and cancel each other’s magnetism. In the case of vacancies, their high concentration makes graphene disintegrate. Professor Geim said: “The observed magnetism is tiny, and even the most magnetized graphene samples would not stick to your fridge. “However, it is an important demonstration that graphene can be magnetized at all.” He then stressed how far the research community has progressed since he and his co-workers first reported graphene isolation in 2004: “One of our dreams is to make graphene magnetic on demand for electronics applications. Now we know the basic rules and it will be a pure fun to play with that.” The discovery brings fundamental new knowledge about how magnetism works at the ultimate limit of one atomic layer and provides researchers worldwide with another toolkit to manipulate graphene’s properties. The most immediate implication is related to switching graphene electronics, but the science involved is much wider. It potentially enables different types of test experiments for theoretical models of magnetism and various low-dimensional magnetic materials that are currently under investigation for future applications such as spintronics devices. texts.full_text: ow the panic of realizing we may not be able to breathe and then also remembering we’re not with anyone who can perform the Heimlich maneuver or CPR. Choking, a silent killer in our midst, claims thousands of lives each year, often under circumstances that could have been prevented by simple awareness and caution. Whether it's choking on a grape at a picnic or a piece of candy while distractedly watching TV, these moments are stark reminders of the importance of eating slowly and mindfully. The consequences can be dire if we're not prepared to handle them ourselves in isolation. We can put a man on the moon, but we can’t be trusted to chew slowly enough to not die. Who knew the stakes were so high every time you ate leftover steak nachos in your kitchen? This is often managed through technologies like dual SIM functionality or eSIM, which allow users to access multiple networks without physically swapping SIM cards. In practice, a multi-mode mobile device can automatically detect the available networks and switch to the one that offers the best signal strength or service quality. This capability is crucial for international travelers who may need to connect to different network standards as they move between countries. For instance, a traveler from the United States, where CDMA is common, might need to connect to a GSM network while visiting Europe. Additionally, advances in mobile technology, such as 5G, are further enhancing the versatility of mobile devices. 5G networks are designed to be more adaptable and can integrate with existing 4G LTE networks, providing even more options for connectivity. texts.full_text: wireless communications systems. Existing DS-CDMA systems are single carrier transmission systems. Two kinds of receivers are generally applied for such DS-CDMA systems namely, Rake receivers and time-domain equalization (TDE) receivers. The performance of these receivers depends on the properties of the wireless environment in which such receivers operate. Because of multipath delay spread of a wireless channel, inter-finger interference (IFI) and multiple access interference (MAI) are inherent with Rake receivers. On the other hand, TDE receivers, though theoretically capable of suppressing IFI and MAI, suffer from slow convergence and complicated computations when applied in DS-CDMA systems. Furthermore, TDE receivers are not able to suppress IFI and MAI effectively in presence of large delay spreads or high system loads which results degrading the overall performance for such cases. In order to overcome these drawbacks we propose a novel receiver architecture based on adaptive filtering techniques combined with multiple access interference cancellation (MAIC) scheme that can significantly improve reception quality by suppressing both IFI and MAI in DS-CDMA systems operating under varying wireless environments including large delay spread conditions or high system loads without requirin

texts.full_text: of the 200 balls, and we are interested in the distribution of the colors of the balls drawn in the sequence. Given the permutation-invariant nature of the draws, a version of the classical de Finetti theorem would suggest that the distribution of the sequence of draws can be approximated by a mixture of product distributions, where each product distribution corresponds to a specific proportion of red and blue balls. In the context of the quantum de Finetti theorem, this idea is extended to quantum states, where permutation-invariant quantum states can be approximated by a probabilistic mixture of product states, as discussed in the abstract of the paper by Ke Li and Graeme Smith. In this scenario, the balls can be thought of as quantum systems, and the act of drawing a ball can be thought of as a quantum measurement. The fully one-way LOCC (local operations and classical communication) measurements mentioned in the abstract can be analogized to the process of drawing balls one by one without replacement, where the measurement (or drawing) of one ball does not affect the classical communication or operations on the previously drawn balls, but the subsequent draws are dependent on the previous ones. This classical analogy helps to build intuition for the quantum case, where the quantum de Finetti theorem provides a powerful tool for understanding and approximating permutation-invariant quantum states, and the applications of this theorem, such as detecting multipartite entanglement and analyzing quantum proof systems, can be be

Both student antors and facilitators will come together at regular intervals over a six week period, during which time there is likely significant progress on all artistic research themes articulated from previous semester's courses. This course seeks to provide the students with critical feedback about their projects. This particular critique class allows for an active exchange of thoughts between artists-working within different disciplines and mediums-and faculty members who are knowledgeable in various fields ranging from design, media studies or philosophy. The process will involve a series of group critiques where each artist presents his/her project-in-progress to the other students & instructors. Each critique session is facilitated by at least one instructor ensuring that all opinions get heard while maintaining an open and productive dialogue. Following this presentation-phase various forms of critical analysis include visual, written or oral feedback can be utilized. The focus of these critiques will not only cover technical aspects such as the development of medium-specific skills but also artistic content. Each critique should encourage artists to think about how their work interacts with and responds thematically to other disciplines in art (or outside) like literature, film or even science. Moreover this session can aid students towards understanding better how one's artwork positions itself within the wider cultural context of its time as well as questioning potential audience reception. Through these critical discussions artists will gain invaluable insights into their artistic practice and research. They are expected to be open-minded about new approaches, suggestions from peers or instructors while keeping engaged in self-reflection on aspects they feel may require further development. The process is designed not only for the critique session itself but also as a long-term reflective learning experience wherein students can synthesize critical feedback with their creative work towards developing more refined artistic outcomes. Once critiques have been completed, each artist will write up an evaluation that recounts key ideas discussed and outlines planned changes or developments to incorporate into future artwork.
